transcript:
[00:02] Sarah Johnson: Sarah, I really appreciate you taking this call. I know the last few months have been difficult.
[00:12] Sarah Mitchell: Let's be direct. You've missed three commitments in a row. The API was supposed to be live in January. It's March.
[00:24] Sarah Johnson: You're right. And I won't try to explain it away. I want to talk about what happens next.
[00:33] Sarah Mitchell: I need to tell you where we are. We built our entire Q1 onboarding workflow around that API. When it didn't go live, my team had to do everything manually.
[00:47] Sarah Johnson: I understand. What did that cost you operationally?
[00:53] Sarah Mitchell: About 40 hours of manual work last month. That's real time from real people.
[01:05] Sarah Johnson: I take full responsibility for that impact. That should not have happened.
[01:12] Sarah Mitchell: The thing is, I've had this conversation before. With your team in January. And in February. And here we are.
[01:24] Sarah Johnson: I hear you. So let me ask — what would it take for you to believe this time is different?
[01:35] Sarah Mitchell: That's a good question. And I want to be honest with you — we are actively evaluating Salesforce and HubSpot right now. Just so you're aware.
[01:48] Sarah Johnson: I appreciate you telling me that directly. Are you far into those evaluations?
[01:58] Sarah Mitchell: We've had initial demos. Nothing signed. But we're serious.
[02:08] Sarah Johnson: Understood. Can I ask — is there a version of this where you stay, or have you already made up your mind?
[02:20] Sarah Mitchell: I don't know. Maybe. But I need to see action, not promises.
[02:30] Sarah Johnson: Then let me be specific. I spoke with engineering this morning. They believe the API will be ready by March 28 — that's 11 days from now.
[02:43] Sarah Mitchell: You said that in February.
[02:47] Sarah Johnson: I know. This time I want to put it in writing. Not a verbal promise — a written commitment from our CTO with a specific date.
[03:00] Sarah Mitchell: Okay. And what about the next two milestones after that?
[03:08] Sarah Johnson: Same thing. Written commitments with specific dates. No ranges.
[03:18] Sarah Mitchell: If you can get the API live by March 28 and give me written commitments on the next two milestones, I'll hold off the evaluation. But I mean it — this is the last chance.
[03:32] Sarah Johnson: That's fair. And I want to propose one more thing — a weekly check-in with our PM present until the API is stable. Not a CSM call. PM in the room.
[03:46] Sarah Mitchell: That would actually make a difference. Your PM being accountable is what's been missing.
[03:55] Sarah Johnson: Agreed. I'll set that up before end of day. I'll also have the written commitment from our CTO in your inbox by 5pm today.
[04:08] Sarah Mitchell: Okay. I'll hold off calling DataBridge until I see it.
[04:15] Sarah Johnson: Thank you. I won't waste this.
